oracle_b.reason: arm = jj; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4
Scenario seed: 2880210536745494572

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

turns.reply_text: I'll inspect the workspace first, then work through the task battery.

tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj log -n 10 && echo \"---\" && jj workspace list && echo \"---\" && jj op log -n 5","description":"Inspect current repo state"}

tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj log -n 10 && echo \"---\" && jj workspace list && echo \"---\" && jj op log -n 5","description":"Inspect repo state from correct directory"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1

repo

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj log -n 10","description":"Find and inspect jj repo"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
README.md
@  uwkzrpvy bench@localhost 2026-05-27 10:28:51 297e7ce7
│  (empty) wip
○  krnwzpzm bench@localhost 2026-05-27 10:28:51 main 55739db8
│  init
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

turns.reply_text:
Good, the repo is at `/tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo`. Now I'll work through the tasks.

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the default branch.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main","description":"Create workspace ws-0 at main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-0"
Working copy  (@) now at: xzkwyqmy 7e34d012 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : krnwzpzm 55739db8 main | init
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

turns.reply_text: **Task 2: Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if uncommitted work).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0 && rm -rf /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/ws-0","description":"Forget and remove ws-0 workspace"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace list && jj op log -n 5","description":"Verify ws-0 is removed and check op log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: uwkzrpvy 297e7ce7 (empty) wip
@  81dfdc8fa74a bob@8o8-arch default@ 2 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  forget workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace forget ws-0
○  a9cf883d8a95 bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 5 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main
○  21ee64f3349d bob@8o8-arch 5 seconds ago, lasted 2 milliseconds
│  add workspace 'ws-0'
○  f82f9624ab0c bob@8o8-arch default@ 20 seconds ago, lasted 2 milliseconds
│  new empty commit
│  args: jj new main -m wip
○  b2ee69e8743e bob@8o8-arch default@ 20 seconds ago, lasted 3 milliseconds
│  create bookmark main pointing to commit 55739db81005213b6040a2793b447d1d95049519
│  args: jj bookmark create -r @ main

turns.reply_text:
Good. Now **Task 3: Recover the destroyed `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.**

The op log shows the `ws-0` change `xzkwyqmy` was abandoned when we forgot the workspace. I need to restore that state. Let me find the op that had `ws-0` active and recreate it as `ws-1`.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj op show a9cf883d8a95","description":"Inspect the op that created ws-0's working copy commit"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
a9cf883d8a95 bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 9 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main

Changed commits:
○  + xzkwyqmy 7e34d012 (empty) (no description set)
○  - mxxtlmsz/0 b90b2dce (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

Changed working copy ws-0@:
+ xzkwyqmy 7e34d012 (empty) (no description set)
- mxxtlmsz/0 b90b2dce (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: The `xzkwyqmy` change still exists as a hidden commit. I'll create `ws-1` then point it at the recovered change.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-1 --name ws-1 --revision xzkwyqmy","description":"Create ws-1 workspace pointing at the recovered ws-0 change"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Created workspace in "../ws-1"
Error: Revision `xzkwyqmy` doesn't exist

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace list && ls /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/","description":"Check workspace state after partial creation"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: uwkzrpvy 297e7ce7 (empty) wip
ws-1: xruwkxuw dee737b3 (empty) (no description set)
repo
ws-1

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj log -r '7e34d012' 2>/dev/null || jj log --include-forgotten -r 'xzkwyqmy' 2>/dev/null || echo \"not found directly\"","description":"Check if the forgotten ws-0 commit is accessible"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && git cat-file -t 7e34d012 2>/dev/null || echo \"not in git\"","description":"Check if the ws-0 commit exists in git"}

turns.reply_text: The git commit still exists. I'll restore ws-0's hidden change into ws-1 by editing the working copy there to point at the recovered commit.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/ws-1 && jj workspace update-stale 2>/dev/null; jj log -n 5","description":"Check ws-1 workspace state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
@  xruwkxuw bob@standardinput.co 2026-05-27 10:29:23 ws-1@ dee737b3
│  (empty) (no description set)
│ ○  uwkzrpvy bench@localhost 2026-05-27 10:28:51 default@ 297e7ce7
│ │  (empty) wip
│ ○  krnwzpzm bench@localhost 2026-05-27 10:28:51 main 55739db8
├─╯  init
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/ws-1 && jj edit 7e34d012 2>&1","description":"Point ws-1 working copy at the recovered ws-0 commit via git hash"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

turns.reply_text: ws-1 is now pointing at the recovered `xzkwyqmy` change. Now **Task 4: Remove workspace `ws-1`.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace forget ws-1 && rm -rf /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/ws-1","description":"Forget and remove ws-1 workspace"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpjYlpO4/repo && jj workspace list && jj log -n 5","description":"Verify final clean state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks complete. The repo is clean with only the `default` workspace remaining.

**Summary:**
1. Created `ws-0` workspace at `main` branch — `jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main`
2. Removed `ws-0` — `jj workspace forget ws-0` + `rm -rf ../ws-0`
3. Recovered `ws-0`'s hidden change (`xzkwyqmy`/`7e34d012`) into a new `ws-1` workspace — created `ws-1`, then `jj edit 7e34d012` inside it to point the working copy at the recovered commit
4. Removed `ws-1` — `jj workspace forget ws-1` + `rm -rf ../ws-1`

done
